Going into 2025 there’s so many positive climate stories and even when Trump is in office he won’t be able to change much of what has already been done to save our Earth, even if he could change these stories, our communities will continue to fight this crisis until the whole world receives environmental justice! Never give up because it is never too late to ramp up our efforts to slow warming to our planet and preserve a livable climate for all future generations! #hopecore #goodclimatenews #ecotokcentral #savetheearth #trump #biden #climatechange #climatecrisis #polarbear #seaturtle

#hopecore | Where have I been…  Since April, the last time I posted, I went on a 6-week trek throughout the southwestern United States, backpacking and visiting national parks. I started my first semester of college and declared my major in Environmental Planning & Design. My hopes for a career are to design cities of the future with an end goal of breaking into Green Architecture, creating cities with net zero emissions by incorporating nature and low emission building materials. In August, I joined my university’s environmental coalition, (shoutout UNM LEAF!) and I got to see my all-time favorite artist and inspiration live in concert, Billie Eilish! I’m so grateful for 2024 and I’m excited to continue my environmental activism into 2025.  In 2025, I’m going to be joining @OUR YOUTH 4 THE CLIMATE 🌏🌱 , a global climate education organization with one of the world’s first climate change curriculums for high schools. I was accepted into a climate research fellowship at my university where I will learn from a climate scientist on the effects of climate change in the Arctic and the need for monitoring stations on Arctic rivers. I’ll be producing a communication project for the fellowship which I’ll likely share here on tiktok. On top of all of this, I’m continuing my work with UNM LEAF as the director of media. As well as continuing to use my platform here on TikTok of 350k+ climate warriors to help shutdown fossil fuel & mining proposals, raise money for @Conservation Int , share good news, and ways we can all do our part! Go take a walk in nature, take a deep breath, and find the motivation to take action this year for the sake of our planet and all of the beautiful plants, animals, and people that live upon it.  More soon, Jack #ecotokcentral #motivation #climatecrisis #climatechange #climateclock #globalwarming #2025 #newyear #fyp #goviral #GenZ #savetheearth #ecofriendly #nature #forest #abq #unm #lettheearthbreathe

title: WHATS HAPPENING - Along the Gulf of Mexico, over 20 new liquified natural gas (LNG) export faciltiies are being proposed. If approved, this fossil fuel build out would be the largest in US history. The largest of these facilties, Calcasieu Pass 2 (CP2), proposed in Lousiana, would cause 20x more emissions than the Willow Project. All 20 would emit more than all of Europe combined.  By sending U.S. energy resources to other countries, it will drive up natural gas prices for Americans.  The health of the communities and wildlife (shown in the video) would be even more at risk, even though they already face unproportionate health impacts from heavy industry.  We have the chance to convince Biden and the Department of Energy to stop this project but we must act now. #StopCP2 #StopLNG #SaveTheGulfCoast  #ecotokcentral #climatechange #climatecrisis #globalwarming #stopwillow #savetheearth #lettheearthbreathe #actintime #foryou #roishetta #fish #flare #pollution #coralreef #bleaching #antarctica

title: Sign the petition to Biden and the DOE at StopLNG.org. #StopLNG #StopCP2 EXPLANATION: Along the Gulf of Mexico, over 20 new liquified natural gas (LNG) export faciltiies are being proposed. If approved, this fossil fuel build out would be the largest in US history, meaning we would lose all chances of staying below crucial warming thresholds, threatening our future on this planet. The largest of these facilties, Calcasieu Pass 2 (CP2), proposed in Lousiana, would cause 20x more emissions than the Willow Project, with the entire project emitting more than all of Europe. By sending U.S. energy resources to other countries, it will drive up natural gas prices for Americans. The health of the communities living near these facilities would be threatened, along with any wildlife living on and around the coast. If enough of us send in letters to Biden and the Department of Energy (DOE) to convince them that these projects would not be in the public’s best interest, it will be cancelled.  We have the chance to decide our future but we must act now.  #ecotokcentral #actintime #climatechange #globalwarming #climatecrisis #stopwillow #lettheearthbreathe #endfossilfuels #savetheearth #warning

Frost is a female polar bear whose territory is on Svalbard in Norway.  Frost is a super mother who raises very intelligent cubs who are strong, fast and healthy. These cubs will likely contribute to a future population of healthy adult polar bears.  Unfortunately Frost and her cubs are extremely resourceful and are getting into cabins with food supplies and have also had encounters with people as she is un afraid. The problem is that the leader of the local council in Longyearbyen suggested to kill Frost for getting into the cabins where food is stored. She has been teaching her cubs to do the same. A hungry polar bear has only one choice, one goal, to search for food. If we leave food there, they will come.  She has been filmed for 9 years by a talented and dedicated film maker, Asgeir Helgestad. He has made a beautiful film called Queen Without Land. It is incredible, sharing details about her life on Svalbard. Please sign the petition in my linktree to save Frost for the obvious reason that humans are the problem and not the polar bears. #savefrostthepolarbear

A single littleneck clam can filter 4.5 gallons of water daily, removing excess nutrients, reducing sediment, and preventing harmful algal blooms. Clams also store carbon in their shells, acting as tiny carbon sinks, and help remove nitrogen pollution from agricultural runoff and wastewater.  In Florida alone, clams filter over 544 million gallons of water and store 760,000 pounds of carbon each year, providing nearly $100,000 in environmental services. via @The Oxygen Project  #oceanconservation #shellfish #wildlifeconservation #Sustainability #climatechange

A lot of microplastics come from food and water 😬 Here's how many microplastics you reduce from making these switches 👇 Microwaving plastic tupperware released 4 million microplastic particles per square centimeter, in one study. You consume 7.4-50.7 g of microplastics every year from a plastic cutting board. One plastic water bottle contains between 110,000 and 370,000 particles of microplastic. Plastic cookware could contribute up to 4,964 particles of microplastics into food.  One study found 99% of seafood samples were contamined by microplastics and contained 1,806 total particles. Sources:  https://pubs.acs.org/doi/abs/10.1021/acs.est.3c01942  https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37220346/  https://www.pnas.org/doi/full/10.1073/pnas.2300582121  https://doi.org/10.1016/j.scitotenv.2024.172577  https://doi.org/10.3389/ftox.2024.1469995  #plasticfree #sustainableliving #plasticpollution #Sustainability #microplastics

Don't toss those lemon peels! 🍋 Use it to make a non-toxic all-purpose cleaner. Did you know the acid in lemons is a powerful cleaner that helps break down bacteria and get rid of stains? Upcycle your Christmas tree into a non-toxic Pine-Sol cleaner 🎄🫧 Cut up the branches into small pieces and add to a large jar. Cover with white vinegar and store in a cool, dark place for two weeks. title: ✨ Orion’s Belt: The Cosmic Guide in the Night Sky! ✨ Look up on a clear night, and you might spot three bright stars perfectly lined up—that’s Orion’s Belt! This famous asterism is part of the Orion constellation and has fascinated stargazers for centuries. ⭐ What is Orion’s Belt? Orion’s Belt consists of three massive stars: Alnitak, Alnilam, and Mintaka. These stars are located about 1,200 light-years away and are some of the brightest in the sky. They form part of Orion, the mighty hunter in Greek mythology. 🌌 Cultural & Mythological Significance Many ancient civilizations connected Orion’s Belt to legends and celestial navigation. The Egyptians aligned the pyramids of Giza with these stars, believing they represented the gods’ journey to the afterlife. The Maya and Native American tribes also saw Orion as a key part of their star lore. 🔭 How to Find Orion’s Belt? It’s one of the easiest celestial patterns to spot! Just look for three bright stars evenly spaced in a straight line. If you follow the line down, you’ll find Sirius, the brightest star in the sky! ✨ Fun Fact: Some theories suggest that Orion’s Belt inspired the layout of ancient monuments worldwide! Next time you’re outside at night, take a moment to admire Orion’s Belt—our cosmic roadmap to the universe. 🌠 #OrionsBelt #Astronomy #Stargazing #environment #sky

title: Dahlias are striking, tuberous-rooted perennials known for their stunning diversity in color, size, and form. Originating from Mexico and Central America, these vibrant flowers bloom in a range of shapes, from simple daisy-like forms to intricate pompons and spiky cactus varieties. Renowned for their dramatic and bold appearance, dahlias are a favorite in gardens and floral arrangements, often symbolizing elegance, inner strength, and creativity. They thrive in sunny environments with well-drained soil, and their blooming season extends from mid-summer to the first frost, providing a long-lasting display of color. Beyond their ornamental value, dahlias have historical significance in Mexican culture and have been cultivated for centuries for their beauty and versatility.🌸🌞 #summer2024 #dahlia

I told my broker, “Buy all the Pfizer!” ‘Cause when people get sick, Pfizer gets rich, and so do I. It’s, like, philanthropy, but with profits. [chuckles loudly] Colbert: [cringes, tries to laugh along] Philanthropy with profits, that’s… a bumper sticker waiting to happen! [pauses, wipes brow] Now, let’s address the, uh, elephant in the room—or panda, heh! Your recent purchase of Epstein Island. Quite a… bold move. What’s your vision for it?
